mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 100 signals a strengthening supply chain that boosts corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5 signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in tightening labor periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million signals a strengthening demand for housing that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 470 billion signals a strengthening demand for consumer goods that boosts corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment above 145 million signals a strengthening labor market that compresses wage growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening labor periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the Consumer Price Index above 243.8 signals a strengthening inflationary environment that boosts interest rates and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 193 billion signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.
